title: SDS316: Data of the US Criminal Legal System
text:
The class is broken into five “sprints,” each having 5 class sessions. Each sprint will follow roughly the same schedule, so that you always know what to expect.
Sprint: 1 lecture - new information! lots of it 2 lab - new information + hands on 3 studnet tech prez + lab - pop quiz! hands on + new information 4 student content prez + lecture - review key concepts / discuss integration (end-of-sprint deliverables DUE)
25 class sessions in the semester draft syllabus

title: SDS316: Data of the US Criminal Legal System
text:
Key deliverables

a list of questions (in class)
A 3 item list of sources to start answering ONE question (committed to class github via pull resquest)
A list of research clusters (submitted by someone from each cluster in a PR - skill: PRs in fast-moving code base / integrating upstream changes)
a lit review from each research cluster, including source datasets
final research questions from everyone (submitted via PR)
initial dataset proof of concept / data description
initial analysis and findings
final findings
narrative

(way down the line)


ethics statement & positionality (how do we relate to ourselves and the data and ourselves via the data)

title: SDS316: Data of the US Criminal Legal System
